meta.description: Authoritative AI-facing registry for Void Energy UI primitives, actions, controllers, utilities, and class recipes.
meta.propsNote: props lists explicit Svelte props. ...rest:* means native element attributes forwarded by spread.
meta.propTypesNote: propTypes appears only when a prop name is not enough for autonomous use. Shared shapes are defined in the top-level types map.
meta.slotsNote: slots lists Svelte 5 snippet props or child content expected by the entry.
meta.contractsNote: controllers, actions, and utilities add methods/contracts when a flat signature string is not enough for autonomous use.
meta.typesNote: types is the compact source index for object unions, callback payloads, and controller state shapes. source points to the canonical local definition.
meta.stateNote: controller state lists reactive fields/getters that consumers may read directly.
rules: There is no generic Button.svelte. Use native <button> with btn-* classes for text-only actions; prefer ActionBtn/IconBtn when an action benefits from an interactive icon.
AI and generation actions should default to Sparkle via ActionBtn/IconBtn unless a more specific interactive icon is a better semantic match.
Use FormField for label, hint, error, and ARIA wiring around form controls.
Modal and toast are manager-driven. Their shells are mounted globally; use the modal/toast APIs instead of rendering new instances.
Prefer native HTML when the platform already provides the right semantics. A missing wrapper does not imply a missing capability.

TileGate.note: Tile accepts TileGate[]. Multiple entries use OR logic.

BarChartGroup.note: BarChartGroupValue = { name: string, value: number, series?: number }.

PasswordValidationState.note: Getter-backed reactive object returned by createPasswordValidation(); do not destructure.

shape: request: { id, targetId, position, fromIndex, toIndex, previousId, nextId, orderedIds }

PartialThemeDefinition.note: VoidEngine normalizes and safety-merges missing keys against a mode-compatible base theme.

search-field.description: Text input with a magnifying-glass icon, optional zoom-expand animation, and debounced input. Submits on Enter.
search-field.compose: Wrap in FormField for label or validation. Use bind:value for reactivity; onsubmit fires on Enter.

edit-field.description: Single-line inline-edit input with confirm/cancel/undo icon buttons that appear on focus. Renders as plain text when idle.
edit-field.compose: Use for single-line inline edits that require explicit confirm or cancel. It owns the edit, undo, and confirm affordances already.

generate-field.description: Single-line text input with an AI sparkle button that triggers async generation. Shows a loading spinner during generation; Escape aborts.
generate-field.compose: Provide an async ongenerate handler that returns the final string. Escape aborts generation.
generate-field.example: <GenerateField bind:value={title} instructions='Generate a project title' ongenerate={generateTitle} />

password-field.description: Password input with a show/hide eye toggle button. Pairs with PasswordMeter and PasswordChecklist for strength feedback.
password-field.compose: Usually pair with FormField plus createPasswordValidation from @lib/password-validation.svelte feeding PasswordMeter and PasswordChecklist.

color-field.description: Native color picker input rendered as a circular swatch with a hex label. Opens the OS color dialog on click.
color-field.compose: Wrap in FormField when you need label, hint, or error wiring. bind:value reflects the native color input.

copy-field.description: Readonly text input with a copy-to-clipboard icon button. Shows brief success feedback after copying.
copy-field.compose: Use for copy-only secrets or IDs. The field is readonly and already handles clipboard feedback.

slider-field.description: Labeled range slider with optional snap-point preset buttons above the track. Displays the current numeric value.
slider-field.compose: Use presets for snap-point selection; without presets it behaves like a labeled native range input.

toggle.description: iOS-style on/off switch with optional custom icons inside the thumb. Renders a native checkbox for form semantics.
toggle.compose: Use for boolean state. Native checkbox semantics and focus handling are already built in.

switcher.description: Segmented pill-bar where one option is highlighted with an animated sliding indicator. Renders native radio inputs for form submission.
switcher.compose: Use when choices are few and mutually exclusive. It renders native radio inputs for form submission.

selector.description: Styled native <select> dropdown with an optional label. Caret icon indicates expandability. Best for long option lists without filtering.
selector.compose: Use when you want native select semantics or a long option list without filtering.
selector.example: <Selector options={fontOptions} bind:value={font} label='Heading Font' placeholder='Select a font...' />

combobox.description: Text input with a floating filtered dropdown list. Supports keyboard navigation, custom values, and async filtering. Floating UI positions the panel.
combobox.compose: Wrap in FormField for label or validation. Use bind:value for committed selection and oninput for async filtering.

drop-zone.description: Dashed-border drop area that accepts dragged files or click-to-browse. Shows file type and size validation feedback via toast.
drop-zone.compose: Use for drag-and-drop uploads with click fallback. Validation and toast errors are already built in.

form-field.description: Label + hint + error wrapper that generates stable IDs and ARIA wiring. The actual input is passed through a children snippet.
form-field.compose: Pass the actual control through the children snippet and forward fieldId, descriptionId, and invalid into it.
form-field.example: <FormField label='Email'>{#snippet children({ fieldId, descriptionId, invalid })}<input id={fieldId} aria-describedby={descriptionId} aria-invalid={invalid} />{/snippet}</FormField>

password-meter.description: Horizontal strength bar that fills and changes color (weak to strong) based on password validation score. Hides when empty.
password-meter.compose: Feed the same createPasswordValidation result used by PasswordChecklist. It hides automatically when password is empty.

password-checklist.description: Vertical list of password rules with check/cross icons that update in real-time as the user types.
password-checklist.compose: Use directly below PasswordField. It expects the validation object returned by createPasswordValidation.

edit-textarea.description: Multiline inline-edit textarea with confirm/cancel/undo icon buttons. Ctrl/Cmd+Enter confirms; Escape cancels.
edit-textarea.compose: Use for multiline inline edits that require explicit confirm or cancel. Ctrl/Cmd+Enter confirms; Escape cancels.

generate-textarea.description: Multiline textarea with an AI sparkle button for async text generation. Shows loading state during generation; Escape aborts.
generate-textarea.compose: Provide an async ongenerate handler that returns the final string. Escape aborts generation while loading.
generate-textarea.example: <GenerateTextarea bind:value={bio} rows={5} instructions='Write a short bio' ongenerate={generateBio} />

action-button.description: Button with an animated icon and optional text label. Icon animates on hover/focus. Use for primary actions like generate, save, or confirm.
action-button.compose: Use when you need a styled button with an animated icon and optional label. Prefer this over hand-rolled <button><Icon />Label</button> markup for generate, retry, confirm, undo, and auth actions. Variant classes go on class.

icon-button.description: Compact icon-only button for toolbars and inline actions. Icon animates on hover. Always provide an aria-label.
icon-button.compose: Use for toolbar or inline icon actions. iconProps can override hover-driven data-state for toggles and timers.

themes-button.description: Button that opens the themes modal. Can render as a full button with label or as a compact icon-only variant.
themes-button.compose: Opens the themes modal via modal.themes(). Use icon={true} to render the btn-icon variant.

profile-button.description: Circular avatar button showing the user's photo or role-based fallback icon. Reads auth state from the user store automatically.
profile-button.compose: Usually use as a Dropdown trigger. It reads auth state from the user store and switches avatar or role badge automatically.

dropdown.description: Floating panel anchored to a trigger button. Positioned via Floating UI. Closes on Escape and click-outside.
dropdown.compose: Use a trigger snippet for the button content and arbitrary panel markup in children. Escape and click-outside handling are built in.

tabs.description: Horizontal tab bar with an animated underline indicator. Supports keyboard arrow navigation and roving tabindex. Content rendered via panel snippet.
tabs.compose: Pass tab metadata and render content through the panel snippet. Keyboard and roving-tabindex behavior are already implemented.

pagination.description: Page number row with prev/next arrows and ellipsis collapsing. Hides when totalPages <= 1. Compact mobile indicator on small screens.
pagination.compose: Use for paged collections. It hides itself when totalPages <= 1 and collapses to a compact mobile indicator automatically.

load-more.description: Intersection-observer trigger that auto-loads the next page when scrolled into view, with a manual 'Load more' button fallback.
load-more.compose: Place after the list or grid. Keep the manual button even when IntersectionObserver auto-loading is enabled.

breadcrumbs.description: Horizontal breadcrumb trail with chevron separators. Active items switch to slash separators for peer-mode navigation.
breadcrumbs.compose: Pass BreadcrumbItem[] with label, href?, and active?. Any active item switches separators to peer-mode slashes.
breadcrumbs.example: <Breadcrumbs items={[{ label: 'Home', href: '/' }, { label: 'Components', href: '/components' }, { label: 'Buttons' }]} />

sidebar.description: Vertical section nav with grouped items and an active highlight. Supports scroll-spy via IntersectionObserver. Renders as a slide-over overlay on small screens.
sidebar.compose: Use for section navigation. It can sync activeId from the URL hash and IntersectionObserver and behaves as an overlay on smaller screens.

settings-row.description: Two-column row with a label on the left and arbitrary controls on the right. Use inside settings screens for consistent alignment.
settings-row.compose: Use inside settings screens to align a label column with arbitrary controls instead of hand-rolling a grid.

pull-refresh.description: Pull-to-refresh wrapper supporting touch, pointer, and mouse wheel inputs. Pulling down reveals a spinner and triggers an async refresh callback. Features rubber-band physics and cooldown.
pull-refresh.compose: Wrap the scrollable page content and return a promise from onrefresh. Use for mobile-style refresh affordances.

skeleton.description: Shimmer placeholder blocks for loading states. Variants: text (single line), avatar (circle), card (rectangle), paragraph (multiple lines).
skeleton.compose: Use shimmer placeholders during loading. paragraph renders multiple text rows automatically.

portal-loader.description: Branded full-page loading state with an animated quill icon and cycling text messages. Use for initial app or portal loading gates.
portal-loader.compose: Use for branded portal loading states. status='loading' shows the animated quill and cycling copy.

loading-text-cycler.description: Rotating text label that cycles through an array of words with a typewriter effect. Use for loading states with dynamic copy.
loading-text-cycler.compose: Use when you need rotating loading copy without building a custom kinetic config.

kinetic-text.description: Text reveal component with animated modes: char-by-char typing, word-by-word, cycling between words (configurable pause), or scramble-decode. Optional cursor, completion callback, and custom HTML tag.
kinetic-text.compose: Use text for char, word, or decode modes and words for cycle mode. It is a thin wrapper around the kinetic action.

media-slider.description: Horizontal volume/playback control bar with mute toggle, play/pause, and optional replay button. Icons animate between states.
media-slider.compose: Use for audio controls. Mute, play/pause, and replay buttons are already wired to animated icons.

progress-ring.description: Circular SVG progress indicator with optional center value label. Themed via series color channels. Animates on mount.
progress-ring.compose: Use for single-metric progress. series maps to themed color channels.

sparkline.description: Tiny inline SVG line chart showing trend shape without axes or labels. Optional fill area beneath the line. Use inside stat cards or table rows.
sparkline.compose: Use inside stat cards, tables, or list rows when you only need trend shape and not full axes.

stat-card.description: KPI card showing label, large value, color-coded trend arrow (up/down/flat), delta percentage, and optional sparkline. Use in dashboard metric grids.
stat-card.compose: Use inside surface-raised grids for KPI dashboards. trend controls the icon and semantic color.

donut-chart.description: Ring chart with clickable segments (keyboard + mouse), optional center metric, legend, tooltips, and series color theming. Segments animate on mount and highlight on hover. Includes accessible data table.
donut-chart.compose: Use for share or distribution views. Pair with a surrounding surface-raised card.

line-chart.description: SVG line chart with axes, grid, tooltips, and optional fill area. Supports single/multi-series data, reference lines, smooth Catmull-Rom curves, data point dots, entry animations, legend, and accessible data table.
line-chart.compose: Use data for a single series or series for multi-series. All series must share labels in the same order.

tile.description: Content card with cover image, title, author link, comma-separated genre text, state mark (resume/completed/replay), and optional token-gate lock overlay. Full-tile click via stretched link.
tile.compose: Use inside a StoryCategory scroll strip. Supports mark states (resume, completed, replay) and gate prop (TileGate[]) for token-gated tiles with lock icon, premium styling, and requirement tooltip (NFT collection, specific NFT IDs, or fungible token balance). Multiple gates use OR logic. Uses the stretched-link pattern for full-tile clickability with an independent author link. Use loading prop for skeleton state.
tile.example: <Tile title='Machine Rebellion' href='/story/123' author={{ name: 'Ada', href: '/user/1' }} genres={['Sci-Fi']} image='/covers/img.jpg' gate={[{ type: 'nft-collection', name: 'Potentials' }]} />

story-category.description: Horizontal scroll strip with category header (title + tagline) and always-visible scroll arrows on tablet+. Designed to hold Tile children. Supports infinite scroll pagination.
story-category.compose: Renders a category header (title + tagline) and a horizontal scroll strip for Tile children. Supports optional pagination: pass loading, hasMore, and onloadmore to auto-append skeleton tiles and trigger loading via IntersectionObserver when the user scrolls near the end.
story-category.example: <StoryCategory title='Hottest right now' tagline='Most played this week.' loading={pageLoading} hasMore={hasNext} onloadmore={fetchPage}><Tile ... /><Tile ... /></StoryCategory>

bar-chart.description: SVG bar chart with axes, grid, tooltips, optional value labels, and reference lines. Supports vertical/horizontal orientation, grouped (clustered) bars, entry animations, legend, and accessible data table.
bar-chart.compose: Use data for simple bars or groups for clustered bars. Negative values are not supported.

create-password-validation.description: Factory that returns a reactive password validation object with strength score, rule checks, and optional confirm-match. Feed into PasswordMeter and PasswordChecklist.
args: options?: { minLength?, maxLength?, allowedChars?, allowedCharsDescription?, requireConfirm? }
returns: Getter-backed reactive object: do not destructure
fields: error, isValid, rules, score, level, hasLower, hasUpper, hasDigit, hasSpecial, hasRestrictedChars, hasValidLength, passwordsMatch
create-password-validation.compose: Create once at component top level and pass the returned object directly into PasswordMeter and PasswordChecklist. Use requireConfirm when the confirm field must match.
create-password-validation.example: import { createPasswordValidation } from '@lib/password-validation.svelte'; const pv = createPasswordValidation(() => password, () => confirm, { requireConfirm: true });

typewrite.description: Imperative function that types text into a DOM element character-by-character. Returns an abortable promise.
typewrite.compose: Use when you need imperative, abortable typing outside Svelte markup or want a promise that resolves when the reveal completes.

is-one-shot-effect.description: Predicate that returns true for narrative effects that fire once (shake, jolt, glitch) vs. continuous ambient effects (breathe, pulse).
is-one-shot-effect.compose: Use to split one-shot narrative punctuation from continuous ambient effects when pairing narrative with kinetic text reveals.

reorder-by-drop.description: Pure function that returns a new array with the dropped item moved to its target position. Use after a between-mode drop event.
args: items: T[] where T extends { id: string }
reorder-by-drop.compose: Use after dropTarget between-mode drops when you only need the reordered collection and not the backend payload.

resolve-reorder-by-drop.description: Like reorderByDrop but also returns a backend-ready payload with fromIndex, toIndex, previousId, nextId, and orderedIds.
args: items: T[] where T extends { id: string }
resolve-reorder-by-drop.compose: Use when a sortable drop must also produce a backend-ready payload describing the move.
resolve-reorder-by-drop.example: const change = resolveReorderByDrop(items, detail); if (change) save(change.request);

tooltip.description: Svelte action that shows a floating text tooltip on hover/focus. Positioned via Floating UI with configurable placement and delay.
tooltip.compose: Use string shorthand for plain text or VoidTooltipOptions when placement, delay, or offset must change.

morph.description: Svelte action that animates an element's width/height when its content changes size. Uses ResizeObserver internally.
morph.compose: Apply to inner containers whose dimensions change with content. Avoid pairing it with CSS width/height transitions on the same node.

kinetic.description: Svelte action for declarative text reveal animations: typing, word-by-word, cycling, or scramble-decode. Restarts on config change.
kinetic.compose: Use for declarative text reveal effects in markup. The action restarts whenever the config object reference changes.

narrative.description: Svelte action for ambient text physics effects: shake, glitch, breathe, pulse, drift, and more. One-shot or continuous loop.
narrative.compose: Use for post-reveal ambient text physics. Pair with isOneShotEffect when only punctuation effects should wait for kinetic completion.

navlink.description: Svelte action that adds a loading data-attribute to links during navigation, enabling CSS loading indicators on click.
navlink.compose: Use on links or navigational surfaces that should expose loading state immediately on primary-button navigation.

draggable.description: Svelte action that makes an element draggable via pointer and keyboard. Supports axis constraints, handle selectors, and drag groups.
draggable.compose: Use with dropTarget for pointer + keyboard drag interactions. Keep IDs stable and group names consistent across the draggable/drop-target pair.
draggable.example: ...

drop-target.description: Svelte action that makes an element a drop zone for draggable items. Supports 'inside' (zone drop) and 'between' (sortable reorder) modes.
drop-target.compose: Use mode='between' for sortable lists and mode='inside' for zone drops. between-mode pairs naturally with reorderByDrop/resolveReorderByDrop.
drop-target.example: ...

modal.description: Global modal manager singleton. Opens confirm, alert, settings, themes, shortcuts, and command palette dialogs. The modal shell is already mounted in Layout.
state: state: { key: 'alert' | 'confirm' | 'palette' | 'settings' | 'shortcuts' | 'themes' | null, props: object, size: 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg' | 'full' }

alert: { title: string, bodyHtml: string } // trusted HTML only
confirm: { title: string, body: string, onConfirm: () => void, onCancel?: () => void, cost?: number }
{ title: string, bodyHtml: string, onConfirm: () => void, onCancel?: () => void, cost?: number } // trusted HTML only

confirm.note: Plain-text body only. Use open('confirm', { bodyHtml, ... }) for trusted HTML.

alert.note: Plain-text body only. Use open('alert', { bodyHtml, ... }) for trusted HTML.

modal.compose: Do not render a new modal shell. Use the modal manager; the global Modal.svelte host resolves fragments from src/config/modal-registry.ts.
modal.example: import { modal } from '@lib/modal-manager.svelte'; modal.confirm('Delete?', 'This cannot be undone.', { onConfirm: removeItem });

toast.description: Global toast notification store. Shows info, success, error, warning, and loading toasts. Supports undo actions, promise tracking, and loading controllers.
toast.compose: Do not render a new toast region. Use the toast store; the global Toast.svelte host renders items and handles timing.
toast.example: import { toast } from '@stores/toast.svelte'; toast.promise(save(), { loading: 'Saving...', success: 'Saved', error: 'Save failed' });

void-engine.description: Runtime theme engine singleton. Manages atmosphere switching, density scaling, user preferences, custom theme registration, and temporary theme previews.

setAtmosphere.note: User-initiated theme switch. Clears any active temporary theme context.

registerTheme.note: Persists the normalized theme into the runtime registry and theme cache.

registerEphemeralTheme.note: Registers a non-persistent runtime theme intended for previews or story scopes.

applyTemporaryTheme.note: Respects userConfig.adaptAtmosphere. Uses the temporary theme stack for restore semantics.

void-engine.compose: Import the singleton; never instantiate your own engine. Prefer setAtmosphere/setPreferences for normal user flows and the temporary-theme API for previews or story-scoped overrides.

layer-stack.description: Global escape-key dismissal stack. Tracks open overlays (modals, dropdowns, sidebars) so Escape closes the topmost layer first.

clear.note: Clears the stack without calling dismiss callbacks.

layer-stack.compose: Prefer higher-level primitives first. Use layerStack only when building a new dismissible surface that must participate in global Escape handling.

shortcut-registry.description: Global keyboard shortcut manager. Registered shortcuts auto-appear in the shortcuts modal. Supports meta and alt modifiers.
shortcut-registry.compose: Register shortcuts from always-mounted components. The shortcuts modal reads shortcutRegistry.grouped automatically, so new entries show up without extra wiring.
shortcut-registry.example: shortcutRegistry.register({ key: 'k', modifier: 'meta', label: 'Command palette', group: 'General', action: () => modal.palette() });

user.description: Auth state singleton with login/logout/refresh, role-based helpers (isAdmin, isCreator, etc.), and local persistence. Drives ProfileBtn automatically.
user.compose: Import the singleton; never create a second user store. It owns auth hydration, local persistence, and the root data-auth DOM contract.

button-native.note: There is no Button.svelte. Native <button> already gets base button styling; use ActionBtn or IconBtn when you need icon animation wiring.

dismiss-button.note: Modal dismiss and cancel actions use btn-ghost btn-error, not a plain ghost button.

surface-raised.spacing: p-lg and gap-lg are the default card recipe
surface-raised.note: Static floating surface for cards and primary containers.

surface-raised-action.spacing: p-md and gap-md are the usual clickable-card recipe
surface-raised-action.note: Interactive floating surface for clickable cards and tiles.

surface-sunk.spacing: p-md and gap-md are the default inset recipe
surface-sunk.note: Recessed surface for inputs, wells, sidebar blocks, and grouped controls.

surface-void.spacing: use only when you need an opaque masking surface
surface-void.note: Opaque canvas surface used by modal and overlay contexts.

surface-spotlight.spacing: p-md and gap-md work well for highlighted rows and callouts
surface-spotlight.note: Slightly lighter solid surface for active sections, emphasized rows, and callouts.

shimmer-surface.note: Loading shimmer treatment for Skeleton or custom placeholders.

page-stack.note: Standard top-level page column used by the homepage and component docs.

section-stack.note: Default vertical section grouping with generous page rhythm.

surface-stack-lg.note: Primary card composition for content blocks and demos.

surface-stack-md.note: Inset grouped-controls composition for nested sections.

inline-actions.note: Responsive action row for buttons, toggles, and chips.

split-grid.note: Standard responsive two-column content or stats layout.

settings-grid.note: Prefer the SettingsRow component instead of hand-rolling label and control alignment.
